Title: Subject
VS Code Explorer Not Showing Files Inside Folder

Description:

Hello Support Team,

I am experiencing an issue in Visual Studio Code where files inside a folder are not visible in the Explorer panel.

In the screen recording I attached, I open my project directory ("week 11") which contains folders like "backend" and "demo". However, when I select or view these folders in VS Code, the files inside them are not displayed.

The video shows that:

The video shows that:

- The folder structure is visible
- But the files inside the folders are missing in the Explorer view
- The same folders exist in File Explorer

I have already tried restarting VS Code and reopening the folder, but the issue still persists.

Could you please help me understand why the files are not appearing and how to fix this?

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
balakrishnankayan999@gmail.com
Balakrishnan m

Thank you for your support.

Best regards,
balakrishnankayan999@gmail.com
Balakrishnan m

Title: Copilot weekly rate limit triggered unexpectedly on Claude Opus 4.6, 4.7 (Pro account)

Description:
Hello GitHub Support,
I am being blocked by a weekly rate-limit message in Copilot Chat, even though I am a paying user and this started unexpectedly.

Observed behavior:

Observed behavior:

Copilot returns HTTP 429 with code user_weekly_rate_limited.
Requests to Claude Opus 4.6 are explicitly marked as rateLimited.
Requests to Claude Opus 4.7 are also marked as rateLimited.
Relevant log evidence (from VS Code Copilot Chat logs on 2026-04-18):

2026-04-18 21:36:41.759 [error] Server error: 429 {"error":{"message":"...","code":"user_weekly_rate_limited"}}
2026-04-18 21:42:39.758 [info] ccreq:4b94bc95.copilotmd | rateLimited | claude-opus-4.6 | 261ms | [panel/editAgent]
2026-04-18 21:30:09.156 [info] ccreq:ffe3e383.copilotmd | rateLimited | claude-opus-4.7 | 230ms | [panel/editAgent]
Please verify:

My Copilot entitlement and premium quota status.
Whether my account is incorrectly classified or capped.
Whether there is a backend issue affecting model access for my plan.
Thank you.

Comments:
This issue is a duplicate of https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/253124. Please refer to that issue for updates and discussions. Feel free to open a new issue if you think this is a different problem.

Title: You've reached your weekly rate limit.

Description:
At this point this tool is unusable. Its shackled and lobotomised.
I am considering pulling my subscription to this as  its becoming something of a burden than usefull.

You can do better but you won't. Some bot will view this and not care.
I am tired of trying to use copilot and all I get is this weekly reset lazy notifications.

Now I have to wait 2 DAMN DAYS!

You can do better but you won't.

DO BETTER.

You can do better but you won't.

DO BETTER.

Title: GitHub Copilot summarization now with no warning and not reversible in v1.116

Description:
It's exhausting filing these summarization/compaction issues (refer to e.g. #289220). This new ticket a degradation of #303549.

Now in v1.116 GitHub Copilot will summarize even with it's only around 76%, even when the remaining space on the meter (even with the reserved space accounted for!) has sufficient room for what I was requesting. At least under #303549 I could see that it was starting to summarize, and cancel the summarization, back up, and perform some other operation that needed the context. Now out of the blue GitHub Copilot will say "summarized" and immediately I lose all context.

Even worse, if I go back in the history, I don't get that summarized context back!! In previous versions, I could cancel compaction and resubmit the previous request, and the resubmission would use the pre-compaction context (i.e. as if compaction had not happened). Now there is no warning and summarization happens instantly. Instant summarization would be fine if I could resend the request with the pre-summarized conversation, but now even if I resubmit the prompt, the pre-compaction context
the pre-summarized conversation, but now even if I resubmit the prompt, the pre-compaction context is gone—the context stays compacted.

It's as if every new version comes out with a further degredation of summarization/compaction.

Look, let me try to break this down in simple terms. Ideally you would provide a hook and let me do my own summarization. If you can't do that, at least do this:

* Have the context full meter level _include_ the reserved space (as it seemed to do before #303549).
* Don't compact the conversation until the meter reaches at least 95%.
* If you compact the conversation, allow me to resubmit the last prompt before compaction using the context that that prompt had before compaction. (i.e. Do not "alter history" by imputing a compacted context backwards in time to a previous prompt's state.)

Those are extremely simple principles! Developers would know what to expect! We could work with the LLM and make informed decision! But now _all three of these simple principles_ are broken!! Why does the behavior have to be so convoluted to deal with, and against user expectations?

```
Version: 1.116.0 (user setup)
Commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679
Date: 2026-04-15T00:28:13Z
Electron: 39.8.7
ElectronBuildId: 13797146
Chromium: 142.0.7444.265
Node.js: 22.22.1
V8: 14.2.231.22-electron.0
OS: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
```
Comments:

One hypothesis of what is going on in v1.116 is that GitHub Copilot realizes that it is running out of space and preemptively compacts the conversation but _does not tell the user about it_ until the subsequent prompt. For example, after I submit Prompt 1, perhaps GitHub Copilot will compact the conversation in the middle of Response 1, but _not tell the user about it_. Thus after Response 1 is finished, the meter still shows space left, but I suspect that the conversation has already been
is finished, the meter still shows space left, but I suspect that the conversation has already been compacted.

This would explain why that when I submit Prompt 2, GitHub Copilot _immediately_ says "Compacted conversation" with no pause or warning —because the conversation has already been summarized in Response 1. It would also explain why resubmitting Prompt 2 does _not_ get the full conversation context back—again because the conversation has already been summarized in Response 1.

So maybe this ticket indicates a new bug in which GitHub Copilot no longer informs the user when conversation is starting to occur, or even immediately after conversation has occurred; and instead still shows a not-yet-full meter and only informs the user of compaction _in the next turn_. Such a bug would explain this new, further degraded behavior.

In pre-v1.116, if GitHub Copilot decided it needed to compact in the middle of a long response, at least it would inform me of that immediately, so that I could cancel summarization, back up, and adjust the prompt or switch prompts altogether (to use a higher-value request that needs the context before it is compacted, for example).
Something is really broken in v1.116. I see this sequence of events frequently—over and over. It's not just a fluke happening every once in a while.

1. The meter will be on 60% or so.
2. I'll have the LLM do some work (such as updating a couple of documents), and the meter will go up to like 73%.
3. Then I'll request that the LLM update a single, small document. I even use the same prompt for updating that document, which I have used hundreds of times in the past, and which should not use more than 1% or 2% of the context window.

**Expected:** The meter will go up to 75%.

**Expected:** The meter will go up to 75%.

**Actual:** The LLM will _immediately_ say "Compacted conversation", and the meter will drop to 20% or so.

This really makes me suspicious that the LLM actually compacted the conversation in Step 2 above, but neglected to tell me compaction was starting, and afterwards showed me a stale meter.
Title: Git worktrees Description: Type: Bug

I had claude create a worktree and make changes in it. the worktree did not show in my source control side pinal repositories list. And when I ran the command Git: Migrate worktree changes it would find the worktree, i clicked on it, and it did not do anything at all. I only got it to work by disabling then re-enabling the git extenstion, and then reloading the window. I was also connected to a dev tunnel at the time but I believe the issue is the same even without the dev tunnel.

Title: Vertical Center on Each "Search for Next Match"

Description:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
If you search for a keyword using VSCode built-in search, when there're multiple matches in the current view of a file (like the window width is very long), pressing F4 to search for the next match will simply change the highlighted block to the next match, but won't scroll the screen, unless the next match is outside of the current view.

However, in GitLens Inspect, to compare a file between two commits, once you press Alt+F5 to jump to the next diff block, the screen will immediately scroll so that the next diff block is at the vertical center of the VSCode window, in terms of width.

Now here's a problem: what if a file is too big for GitLens Inspect to load? In that case, I have to use `git diff` command in Git Bash to export the diff to a file, open that file in VSCode, and then search for the regular expression "^[^-+].*\n[+-]" in this diff file to quickly jump between diff blocks. However, the pain is, once I press F4 to jump to the next match of this regular expression, the VSCode window doesn't set the next diff block to be at the vertical center, but instead just

the VSCode window doesn't set the next diff block to be at the vertical center, but instead just change the highlighted block to the next match. Because this action is different from that of GitLens Inspect, I have to adjust myself to this habit.

So, I'm wondering if you could add an option, like "Center on Search", so that when this option is checked, each time I search for the next match, the screen will always be scrolled so that the next match will be at the vertical center of the window? I'd appreciate it if you could take this into consideration.

Comments:
Supplement: I just found that by pressing Ctrl+G, one can jump to a specific line. That specific line is always put in the vertical center of the VSCode window. So if you plan to add this feature, you can just use the logic from "Go To Line" function.

And on top of this, would you please enable users to adjust the location where the line would end? I've been stressing vertical center (which means 50% from the top or bottom) above, but is that possible to implement 40%, 30% from the top or bottom, or even -10%, +110%, etc., so that the user can decide?
